parityReductionStatus.label: unitary parity reduction
parityReductionStatus.reason: Reflection symmetry decomposes the original coordinate Weyl form as the orthogonal direct sum of even and odd half-line forms.  This is a unitary change of variables.

primitiveMixedDerivativeStatus.label: mixed-derivative primitive transport
primitiveMixedDerivativeStatus.reason: Boundary decay gives K_pm(x,y)=int_x^infty int_y^infty H_pm(u,v) du dv.  Fubini transports finite coordinate Weyl tests to primitive/cumulative mixed-kernel tests without changing the quadratic form value.

volterraNormalizationStatus.label: Volterra/log normalization
volterraNormalizationStatus.reason: The log coordinate and multiplication by positive Psi weights are invertible form-domain isomorphisms.  They preserve nonnegativity and identify the transported mixed kernel with the normalized Volterra quotient form.

formCoreClosureStatus.label: form-core closure
formCoreClosureStatus.reason: The primitive compact core is dense in the transported closed Volterra form domain.  The parity, primitive, and Volterra maps are continuous in the closed graph norms, so the exact identity extends from finite tests to the completed form domain by closed-form continuity.

quotientSchurStatus.label: normalized quotient Schur positivity
quotientSchurStatus.reason: The imported Weyl/Volterra quotient Schur theorem proves the nonnegative closed quotient form in the normalized full-Phi Volterra model.

primitiveEndpointCompatibilityStatus.label: primitive endpoint compatibility
primitiveEndpointCompatibilityStatus.reason: The primitive endpoint compatibility theorem proves that the trace correction produced by the quotient transport is annihilated on the primitive image: D_bdy=0 and D_q=0 on the transported trace range.

formalProof: Use reflection symmetry to split the original Weyl form into even and odd half-line sectors.
Use the mixed-derivative primitive identity and Fubini to transport coordinate tests to primitive mixed-kernel tests.
Apply the positive Volterra/log normalization to identify the transported form with the normalized quotient form.
Extend the identity from the primitive compact core to the closed form domain by density and closed-form continuity.
Apply the quotient Schur theorem for positivity on the normalized quotient form.
Apply primitive endpoint compatibility to show the trace-side boundary correction is annihilated, so the transported quotient positivity is exactly the original Weyl positivity.
numericalEvidenceRole: Finite approximation diagnostics in older detailed lift ledgers are audit evidence only; this theorem uses the closed quotient Schur and primitive endpoint compatibility theorems as analytic inputs.
nextProofTarget: Use this theorem as the lift input for the uniform omega Weyl/KLM bridge.
